persona_name: Alex and Jordan — Craft Beer and Hiking Couple

bugs.title: Shared trip view returns 0 itinerary items
bugs.description: When viewing a shared trip via GET /shared/{token}, the response includes the trip metadata but the itinerary days array contains 0 items, even though the trip has a full itinerary (19 items for CZ, 7 for Iceland). The itinerary data appears to not be included in the shared view response.
bugs.expected: Shared trip view should include itinerary items so the recipient can see the planned activities
bugs.actual: itinerary.days[].items is empty for both shared trips despite having 19 and 7 items respectively

bugs.title: ListChatSessions shows messageCount=0 for sessions with messages
bugs.description: ListChatSessions returns messageCount=0 for sessions that have confirmed messages (verified via GetChatHistory which returned 4 messages for the CZ trip session). This may be a Firestore aggregation issue or intentional optimization, but is misleading for UI.
bugs.expected: messageCount should reflect actual message count
bugs.actual: messageCount is 0 for sessions with 4+ messages

bugs.title: Duplicate Zatec itinerary items on Day 4
bugs.description: The AI created two overlapping items for Zatec on Day 4: 'Zatec Hop Museum & Temple of Hops and Beer' (order 1) and 'Zatec: The Hop Pilgrimage' (order 1). Both are order_in_day=1 and describe essentially the same activity (visiting the hop museum). This appears to be a tool-loop artifact where the AI created itinerary items in two separate tool calls with overlapping content.
bugs.expected: One consolidated Zatec museum/hop pilgrimage item
bugs.actual: Two near-duplicate items with same order_in_day=1

bugs.title: Usage count does not include selection-mode messages
bugs.description: Sent 4 total AI messages (1 selection CZ + 1 planning CZ + 1 selection Iceland + 1 companion CZ), but usage shows 3. The initial selection-mode message for CZ may not have been counted, or the selection-mode Iceland message was counted with the planning session. Minor discrepancy.
bugs.expected: Usage count should match total SendMessage calls

ux_issues.description: The sour beer planning message (message 2 in CZ planning) did not appear in GetChatHistory for the CZ trip session. It may have been stored under a different session context since it was sent via CHAT_MODE_PLANNING with the same session_id but the initial trip creation happened in CHAT_MODE_SELECTION. The session spanning across modes may cause message gaps in history.
ux_issues.suggestion: Ensure all messages sent to the same session_id are retrievable via GetChatHistory regardless of mode transitions

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Duplicate itinerary items created in tool loop iterations
ai_behavior_issues.context: On the CZ trip, the AI created two near-identical Zatec items in Day 4 (both with order_in_day=1). This suggests the AI called create_itinerary_items in multiple tool loop iterations without deduplicating against what it had already created.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: This was an exceptionally impressive test run. The extended profiles for CZ and IS loaded perfectly — the Czech expert 'Jakub' demonstrated deep knowledge of Czech brewing traditions including obscure breweries (Unetice, Wild Creatures, Fenetra), cultural customs (Mliko pour, coaster rules), and niche sour/wild ale recommendations. The Iceland expert 'Bjorn' provided detailed Laugavegur stage-by-stage breakdown with distances, elevation, timing, and terrain, plus critical logistics about hut booking urgency, river crossings, and weather. The two-trip workflow was completely clean — creating Iceland did NOT interfere with the Czech trip. Companion mode response was appropriately concise and location-specific. The craft-beer and hiking extended themes combined with CZ and IS extended locations produced genuinely expert-level personas. As a craft beer and hiking enthusiast, this would be genuinely useful for trip planning.
